content:
Key Findings

  • Fylde Borough Council spent £157.4M across 0 transactions between 2015/16 and 2025/26
  • The council transacted with 1,689 unique suppliers during this period
  • The highest spending supplier was BLACKPOOL COUNCIL with £15.9M
  • The top supplier by spend, NEW FYLDE / PROGRESS HOUSING LTD, received £886K
  • All top suppliers had a risk score of 0

Understanding Supplier Risk

Fylde Borough Council's spending data reveals a comprehensive view of supplier relationships over the past decade. The council transacted with 1,689 unique suppliers, reflecting a diverse range of services and goods procured to support local operations. Notably, the total spend analysed amounted to £157.4M, highlighting the significant financial activity managed by the council.

Supplier risk scoring is a critical aspect of financial transparency. It helps identify potential vulnerabilities in the supply chain, ensuring that public funds are used efficiently and effectively. In Fylde's case, all top suppliers had a risk score of 0, indicating a low-risk profile. This suggests that the council has implemented robust procurement practices to mitigate potential risks.

Top Suppliers and Their Impact

The data shows that BLACKPOOL COUNCIL was the highest spending supplier with £15.9M. This substantial investment underscores the interdependence between local authorities in delivering public services. The next highest supplier, NEW FYLDE / PROGRESS HOUSING LTD, received £886K, highlighting the council's commitment to housing and community development.

Other notable suppliers include WHITTAKERS OF BLACKPOOL LTD with £310K and HELEN SHEARN ASSOCIATES with £80K. These suppliers provide a range of services, from event hire to consultancy, demonstrating the breadth of Fylde's procurement activities. The consistent risk score of 0 across these suppliers indicates a stable and reliable supply chain.

Analysing Supplier Risk Scores

Supplier risk scores are calculated based on various factors, including financial stability, compliance history, and service reliability. A score of 0 signifies minimal risk, suggesting that these suppliers have a strong track record of delivering on their contracts. This is particularly important for public sector spending, where accountability and transparency are paramount.

The absence of high-risk suppliers in Fylde's top spenders list is a positive indicator of the council's procurement strategy. It reflects a proactive approach to risk management, ensuring that public funds are allocated to reliable and trustworthy suppliers. This not only safeguards the council's financial interests but also enhances the quality of services delivered to residents.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Fylde Borough Council about the criteria used for supplier risk assessment
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about procurement policies and practices
  • Check the council's published contracts register to explore detailed spending information
  • Write to your local councillor about the importance of maintaining low-risk supplier relationships
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and gain deeper insights into Fylde's financial activities
This article is based on Fylde Borough Council's published spending data covering 2015/16 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
